assertions.reason: `agent -h` printed the usage/help block in the terminal and the appended `echo` reported `__EXIT:0`.
`agent --help` printed the same usage/help block and the appended `echo` reported `__EXIT:0`.
The help output visibly included `agent <cwd> <allowed-commands> <prompt>`, the `<cwd>`, `<allowed-commands>`, and `<prompt>` argument labels, plus `--model <id>` under Options.
The help block included three concrete `agent ...` example invocations with quoted prompts.
`agent -h . "*" "ignored prompt"` still rendered help instead of spawning work, and the appended `echo` reported `__EXIT:0`.
`agent . "*" "prompt" --help` still rendered help with no spawn output, and the appended `echo` reported `__EXIT:0`.
Bare `agent` printed `agent: missing required argument <cwd>` in the terminal and the appended `echo` reported `__EXIT:1`.
Page evaluation returned `typeof globalThis.__slicc_agent === "object"` and `typeof globalThis.__slicc_agent.spawn === "function"`.

interactiveChecks.action: Entered `agent -h; echo "__EXIT:$?"` in the SLICC terminal.
interactiveChecks.observed: The terminal printed the full help block with usage, arguments, options, and examples, then printed `__EXIT:0`.

interactiveChecks.action: Entered `agent --help; echo "__EXIT:$?"` in the SLICC terminal.
interactiveChecks.observed: The terminal printed the same help block and then printed `__EXIT:0`.

interactiveChecks.action: Entered `agent -h . "*" "ignored prompt"; echo "__EXIT:$?"` in the SLICC terminal.
interactiveChecks.observed: The help flag won over the positional arguments, the help block rendered again, and the terminal printed `__EXIT:0`.

interactiveChecks.action: Entered `agent . "*" "prompt" --help; echo "__EXIT:$?"` in the SLICC terminal.
interactiveChecks.observed: A trailing `--help` still rendered the help block and the terminal printed `__EXIT:0`.

interactiveChecks.action: Entered `agent; echo "__EXIT:$?"` in the SLICC terminal.
interactiveChecks.observed: The terminal printed `agent: missing required argument <cwd>` and `__EXIT:1`.

interactiveChecks.action: Evaluated `typeof globalThis.__slicc_agent` and `typeof globalThis.__slicc_agent.spawn` in the running page.

frictions: The preferred `ws://localhost:5710/cdp` proxy intermittently returned `No page found`; I completed the checks by attaching agent-browser directly to the discovered Chrome CDP port `60583`, which still exercised the same shared dev browser surface.

Opening `http://localhost:5710` in a fresh agent-browser session landed on the provider dialog instead of the live workspace, so the smoke test had to attach to the already running shared Chrome session rather than using a new isolated browser window.
